title: Latremoliere propinquity assembly on enlarged operator system

diagnostic_beta1_vs_L5.beta1_quantity: max_L_op^flip / max_L_op^nat ratio scaled to Lambda^P45
diagnostic_beta1_vs_L5.structural_reading: beta.1 heuristic measures per-generator Lipschitz numerator; L5 actual measures state-space propinquity bounded by Berezin reach + C_3-controlled height.  These are different quantities; beta.1's factor-of-3 is the substrate-enlargement of L_op, L5's factor-of-1 is the rate-survival of gamma under enlarged G.  Both are correct answers to different questions.

vs_paper46: Direct-sum Berezin B^enlarged = B^nat + B^flip (new construction)
prop_achievable = 1 (vs Paper 46's prop = 2) - categorically denser generating set
Strict-strong-form on FULL Krein space (not K+-restricted)
Positivity property (a) REFINED to f^flip = 0 sub-case (structural feature)
share_with_paper46: gamma rate = gamma^natural at saturating f (bit-exact)
Lambda values bit-equal at panel cells (this sprint)
structural_novelty.novelty_score: MEDIUM - genuinely new construction and substrate, but bit-exact propinquity bound match limits publishable novelty

elegance.verdict: HIGH - the strict-strong-form separation manifests in the seminorm (L_op(a^flip) > 0) but is paid in the gradient norm (G^enlarged absorbs flip content), leaving the propinquity bound unchanged.
elegance.structural_reading: Strong-form separation lives at L_op level, gets paid by gradient extension, propinquity bound is invariant - the cleanest possible upgrade path

for_standalone_paper47: First strict-strong-form Lorentzian propinquity (no K+ restriction)
First operator-system construction with prop = 1 (vs Connes-vS Toeplitz prop = 2)
J-graded gradient norm is a structural innovation
Genuinely new substrate / construction even if Lambda bound matches
against_standalone: Lambda bit-equal to Paper 46 - 'free upgrade' undermines publishable novelty of bound
Best phrased as 'Paper 46 extends to full Krein space at no propinquity cost'
Risk of journal pushback: 'isn't this Paper 46 plus a section?'

task4_paper47_verdict.recommendation_rationale:
Three structural reasons support (b) over (a) and (c):
  1. Lambda bit-exact match means there's no quantitative novelty
     beyond Paper 46 in the propinquity bound; the substantive new
     content is the construction (J-graded gradient, direct-sum
     Berezin), which fits cleanly as a Paper 46 §6 extension/appendix.
  2. The 'free upgrade' reading is *more* elegant when presented as
     'Paper 46 transports to the strict-strong-form on the full Krein
     space at no propinquity cost' than as a separate Paper 47 with
     a bit-equal main theorem.
  3. Operationally, drafting Paper 47 risks confusing the reader
     (why two papers with the same Lambda values?); a Paper 46
     extension keeps the narrative coherent.

If the PI nonetheless prefers (a), the right framing for Paper 47
would be: 'Strict-strong-form Lorentzian propinquity convergence on
the enlarged operator system - a structural extension of Paper 46.'
The headline would be the structural unification (J-graded gradient
absorbs strict-strong-form content; propinquity bound preserved),
not a new Lambda value.

(c) Reframe Paper 46: NOT RECOMMENDED.  Paper 46 is already drafted
as a Riemannian-substrate result, and reframing it post-hoc to
include the enlarged substrate as its main result would risk
obscuring its current contribution.

summary.verdict: Lambda^enlarged = Lambda^P45 = Lambda^P46 bit-exact at panel
summary.deepest_finding: The strict-strong-form separation manifests at the Lipschitz-seminorm level (L_op(a^flip) > 0 vs L_P45^+(a^flip) = 0) and is paid entirely in the gradient-norm extension G^enlarged.  The Latremoliere propinquity bound is invariant under this trade: the substrate enlarges, the gradient norm enlarges, the rate gamma^joint and constant C_3 are unchanged at saturating test functions, the assembled C_5^joint = 1 is preserved, and Lambda is unchanged.  This is the deepest 'free upgrade' reading.
